1.title: Data explorer results
1.query:
timeseries query_count = sum(dt.sfm.active_gate.kubernetes.api.query_count),
  by: { dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster }
| fieldsAdd kubernetes_cluster_name = entityName(dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster)
| sort arraySum(query_count) desc

2.title: Data explorer results
2.query:
timeseries query_count = sum(dt.sfm.active_gate.kubernetes.api.query_count, rollup: avg),
  by: { dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster, path }
| fieldsAdd kubernetes_cluster_name = entityName(dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster)
| sort arraySum(query_count) desc
| limit 10

3.title: Data explorer results
3.query:
timeseries query_duration = avg(dt.sfm.active_gate.kubernetes.api.query_duration, rollup: avg),
  by: { dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster }
| fieldsAdd kubernetes_cluster_name = entityName(dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster)
| sort arrayAvg(query_duration) desc

4.title: Data explorer results
4.query:
timeseries query_count = sum(dt.sfm.active_gate.kubernetes.api.query_count),
  by: { dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster }
| fieldsAdd kubernetes_cluster_name = entityName(dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster)
| sort arraySum(query_count) desc

5.title: Data explorer results
5.query:
timeseries query_count = sum(dt.sfm.active_gate.kubernetes.api.query_count, rollup: avg),
  by: { dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster, access_type, path, status_code }
| fieldsAdd kubernetes_cluster_name = entityName(dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster)
| sort arraySum(query_count) desc

6.content:
## Access Type

- **ApiServer:** This access type indicates that a call used the Kubernetes API Server. This could be a normal API resource request but also a call via kube-proxy.

- **DirectIp:** The internal IP address of the requested resource was used for the call. This can either be a pod or a node. This access type can only be applied if you run the ActiveGate inside the kubernetes cluster.
***
## Status Codes
The following list gives you some hints for investigation based on the most common status codes.


- **-1: Internal error.** Dynatrace could not construct the URL. **Prometheus:** The [pod annotations](https://dt-url.net/y942vsf#annotate-prometheus-exporter-pods) might be incorrect.

- **401: Unauthorized.** If you connect via URL and Token, please doublecheck that the values are correct.

- **403: Forbidden.** The service account might lack [required permissions](https://dt-url.net/un02v1h).

- **404: Not Found.** The resource does not exist. This can happen when resources were deleted. **Prometheus:** It is likely that the [`metrics.dynatrace.com/path`](https://dt-url.net/y942vsf#path-to-metrics-endpoint) annotation points to a non-existent endpoint.

- **503: Service Unavailable.** The Kubernetes API experiences an outage.
  **Prometheus:** The [`metrics.dynatrace.com/port`](https://dt-url.net/y942vsf#metrics-port) annotation might contain a wrong value. Another common reason for this status code are wrong tls settings.
***
## Further Resources
1. [General overview about HTTP Status Codes: RFC7231](https://dt-url.net/pp62vxa)

2. [Monitor Prometheus metrics](https://dt-url.net/y942vsf)
